tossups.question: The aim of this series is often said to be the “revelation and evocation of vision” after a phrase from its artist’s 8-line essay “The Origin of Art.” This series was developed from paintings inspired by adobe houses the artist saw on trips to the Southwest, the Variant series. This series influenced the characteristic style of its artist’s student Richard Anuszkiewicz. The Met is home to entries in this series such as Precinct, Soft Spoken, and With Rays. Although the tutelage of Sewell Sillman and Norman Ives allowed the artist to make some entries as screenprints, most entries in this series were created by applying paint with a palette knife to the rough side of Masonite boards. Created from (*) 1949 to 1976 while its artist taught at Black Mountain College and Yale University, this series consists of paintings of three or four nested shapes of various colors. For 10 points, name this series of paintings of quadrangles by Josef Albers.

This series influenced the characteristic style of its artist's student Richard Anuszkiewicz. The Met is home to entries in this series such as Precinct, Soft Spoken, and With Rays. Although the tutelage of Sewell Sillman and Norman Ives allowed the artist to make some entries as screenprints, most entries in this series were created by applying paint with a palette knife to the rough side of Masonite boards. Created from (*) 1949 to 1976 while its artist taught at Black Mountain College and Yale University, this series consists of paintings of three or four nested shapes of various colors. bonuses.leadin: Works in this series of paintings were created by applying oil paints to Masonite boards with a palette knife. For 10 points each:
parts: Identify this series of minimalist experiments in color theory by Josef Albers that consist of nested shapes.
An "homage" to this artist by Maurice Denis shows members of Les Nabis (NAH-bee) surrounding one of this artist's still lifes. This artist is also known for his painting of Mont Sainte-Victoire.

This artist depicted a naked woman "Contemplating the Mediterranean Sea" in a painting subtitled Homage to Rothko that depicts Abe Lincoln when viewed from afar. His painting alternately titled Homage to Crick and Watson has an original title that is a portmanteau of his wife's name and deoxyribonucleic acid.

parts_sanitized: Identify this series of minimalist experiments in color theory by Josef Albers that consist of nested shapes.

bonuses.leadin: Answer some questions on the use of a certain geometric shape in art, for 10 points each:
parts: Each entry in this series by Josef Albers uses a different colour palette to show four of the namesake shapes stacked on top of one another.
This artist only needed to paint one quadrilateral in his Black Square, an emblem of the Suprematist movement.

Though none of the shapes in the work is technically a square, Jean Arp created the “chance collage” Squares Arranged According to the Laws of Chance by dropping fifteen pieces of torn paper onto a larger sheet. Arp was a part of this movement, named for the French for “hobby-horse”.
